product_direction: Add scheduled CSV export to the reports page for team admins.

target_population: Team admins who repeatedly export reports
excluded_groups: Teams without recurring report export workflows

stakeholder_effects.effect: Scheduled exports could change how recurring reporting work is coordinated.

human_owned_decisions: Whether recurring export is strategically worth building

approval_gates.condition: Before any scheduling infrastructure build starts.

unresolved_assumptions: Whether the request reflects a recurring workflow or one-off audits.
strongest_signal: Fourteen support tickets in one quarter ask for recurring report export.
riskiest_claim: Admins will schedule exports instead of downloading manually.
main_uncertainty: Whether the request reflects a recurring workflow or one-off audits.
main_trade_off: Verify the recurring need before building scheduling infrastructure.

user.strongest_signal: Admins already download the same report every week.
user.weakest_assumption: The weekly download is done by the same person each time.
user.missing_evidence: Who consumes the exported file downstream.
user.disconfirming_evidence: Downloads are irregular one-off audit pulls.
user.stop_condition: No admin can name a recurring consumer of the export.

evidence.strongest_signal: Ticket volume is consistent across three months.
evidence.weakest_assumption: Ticket requests represent the broader admin base.
evidence.missing_evidence: Product logs showing repeat manual export behavior.
evidence.disconfirming_evidence: Logs show most exporters export only once.
evidence.stop_condition: Export behavior cannot be measured per account.

business.strongest_signal: Scheduled exports deepen weekly workflow dependency.
business.weakest_assumption: Maintenance cost of scheduling stays bounded.
business.missing_evidence: Infrastructure cost of recurring export jobs.
business.disconfirming_evidence: Exports are replaced by a BI integration request.
business.stop_condition: Scheduling requires a new permission or billing model.

next_smallest_action: Query product logs for repeat manual exports per account over 90 days.

may_do: Analyze existing export logs and interview two weekly exporters.
must_not_do: Build scheduling infrastructure or change report permissions.
must_ask: The proof step requires production schema changes or new permissions.

human_approval_gates.condition: Before any scheduling infrastructure build starts.

stop_conditions: Fewer than ten accounts export the same report more than twice.
The proof step requires production schema changes.
proof_step: Measure repeat manual export behavior in product logs over the last 90 days.
